frameworks.name: subjective inflation
frameworks.one_line: Peripheral percepts feel more vivid than the underlying representation supports

frameworks.name: flashed face distortion effect
frameworks.one_line: Extends the face-distortion illusion paradigm into the color domain

frameworks.name: liberal detection bias in unattended periphery
frameworks.one_line: Cited to relate inflation to known peripheral signal-detection biases

core_question: Does temporal overload in the periphery inflate perceived color saturation?
key_assumptions: Subjective vividness can dissociate from objective peripheral representation
Rapid temporal flashing imposes processing overload analogous to face-distortion paradigm
Perceived saturation can be measured behaviorally as the dependent variable
manipulations: Preceding peripheral color patches with rapid flashes of other color patches
Spatially peripheral vs more central presentation
key_findings: Peripheral color patches were perceived as more saturated under temporal overload
Effect was modest in magnitude but statistically significant
Effect replicated in a second experiment
interpretations: Subjective inflation of color saturation occurs in the periphery under temporal-spatial overload
Inflation relates to previously reported liberal detection biases in the unattended periphery
